auth.totpPasswordChangeDescription: Enter your current TOTP code to apply the new password.
auth.totpDisableDescription: Enter your current TOTP code to disable two-factor authentication.
auth.totpRotateCurrentDescription: Enter your current TOTP code to continue secret rotation.
auth.totpSetupDescription: Scan the QR code in your authenticator app and enter the 6-digit code.
auth.totpLoginDescription: Enter the code from your authenticator app to finish login.
auth.totpQrHint: If the QR code does not scan, use the manual secret.
auth.totpPendingExpired: Verification session expired, please sign in again
auth.tooManyAttempts: Too many login attempts. Try again in 15 minutes.
auth.tooManyTotpAttempts: Too many verification attempts. Try again later.
auth.totpLoginLockoutMessage: Login is temporarily blocked due to too many invalid TOTP codes.
auth.enterPasswordAndTotp: Enter current password and TOTP code
auth.totpRequiredForRotate: TOTP must be enabled to rotate secret

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.restoreConfirm:
Restore database from "{filename}"?

⚠️ Current data will be replaced!

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.sniHint: Custom SNI for client connections (domain fronting). Only works with self-signed certificates (no domain). If domain is set, SNI must match it
nodes.portRangeHint: Clients will connect to these ports
nodes.groupsHint: Users of these groups will have access to this node. If not selected — available to all.
nodes.sshPrivateKeyHint: Paste your private key (OpenSSH or PEM format). Leave empty to keep current.
nodes.generateSshKeyHint: Generates an ed25519 keypair and installs the public key on the server via SSH (requires password or existing key).
nodes.generateSshKeyWarning:
Generate and install a new SSH key on this node?

This will:
• Generate a new ed25519 keypair
• Add the public key to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys
• Save the private key to the panel (encrypted)

The existing password will remain unchanged.
nodes.downloadSshKeyMissing: SSH private key is not configured for this node
nodes.maxOnlineHint: 0 = unlimited. If exceeded, node is hidden from subscription (if enabled).
nodes.rankingHint: Lower = higher in subscription. Drag-and-drop on nodes page will overwrite this value
nodes.lbDragHint: Load balancing is active — drag sets tiebreaker priority when nodes have equal load
nodes.customConfigHint: If enabled — this config will be used during sync instead of auto-generation
nodes.customConfigFormat: YAML format. Use 'Generate' button to fill with base config.
nodes.cascadeRoleHint: Defines the node's role in the cascade topology. Bridge nodes are hidden from user subscriptions.
nodes.rolePortal: Accepts clients and tunnels traffic through the cascade
nodes.roleBridge: Internet exit point, hidden from user subscriptions
nodes.roleRelay: Intermediate hop, forwards traffic further down the chain
nodes.countryHint: Country code for grouping on the network map
nodes.bridgeNodeHint: Bridge nodes are NOT visible in user subscriptions. Auto Setup will only install Xray (no Agent, no Reality). The actual bridge config is deployed when you create a cascade link.
nodes.portalNodeHint: Portal node — users connect here. Auto Setup works as usual (Xray + Agent + Reality). After setup, create a cascade link to connect to a Bridge node.
nodes.autoSetupConfirm:
Setup node automatically via SSH?

This will:
• Install Hysteria (if not installed)
• Generate certificate
• Upload configuration
• Setup port hopping
• Restart service
nodes.autoSetupConfirmXray:
Setup node automatically via SSH?

This will:
• Install Xray-core (latest version)
• Generate Reality keys (if needed)
• Install CC Agent (no-SSH management)
• Configure firewall
• Start services
nodes.setupRunning: Setup in progress... This may take 1-2 minutes.
nodes.configHint: Copy this config to /etc/hysteria/config.yaml on the server:
nodes.loadConfigConfirm: Load current config from node via SSH?
nodes.confirmDeleteFull: Are you sure you want to delete this node?
nodes.ipAlreadyExists: A node with this IP already exists
nodes.nameAndIpRequired: Name and IP address are required
nodes.sectionObfuscationHint: Salamander hides QUIC traffic from DPI. Enable only if your ISP blocks Hysteria — may perform worse on mobile networks. Note: when enabled, the server is no longer compatible with standard QUIC and no longer a valid HTTP/3 server.
nodes.acmeDnsProviderHint: Available provider names are listed in ACME DNS config docs.
nodes.acmeDnsConfigHint: Raw JSON object for provider-specific credentials/options.
nodes.sectionMasqueradeHint: Masquerade modes from docs: `proxy` and `string` (`file` mode is intentionally not exposed in UI).
nodes.masqueradeProxyInsecure: Disable TLS verification for the proxied website
nodes.masqueradeStringHeadersHint: One header per line: key: value
nodes.sectionBandwidthHint: Server-side upload/download limits and client limit policy.
nodes.sectionSpeedTestHint: Enables built-in speed test responses for Hysteria clients.
nodes.sectionUdpHint: UDP forwarding behavior and idle timeout.
nodes.sectionSniffQuicHint: Advanced options from Full Server Config.
nodes.sniffRewriteDomain: Rewrite requests that are already domain-based
nodes.sniffPortsHint: Same format as Port Hopping: comma-separated ports/ranges, or `all`.
nodes.sectionResolverHint: DNS resolver settings used by the server.
nodes.sectionAclHint: Use either `inline` or `file` (not both). GeoIP/GeoSite options are optional.
nodes.aclInlineRulesHint: One rule per line. If empty, legacy default ACL rules are used.
nodes.aclRulesEditorHint: Inline ACL rules are edited on the
nodes.domainHintHysteria: If set — TLS certificate will be issued automatically (ACME). Also used in subscription URLs instead of IP.
nodes.sniHintHysteria: Custom SNI for clients. Works ONLY without domain (self-signed cert). If domain is set — this field is ignored.
nodes.outboundsAclLockedDisabled: Outbounds ACL editor is unavailable because ACL is disabled for this node.
nodes.hintDomain: Domain for SNI and ACME certificates
nodes.hintPortRange: UDP port range for port hopping (e.g., 20000-50000)
nodes.hintRanking: Lower value = higher priority in subscription
nodes.sshNotConfigured: SSH not configured. Auto-setup and terminal require SSH credentials.
nodes.xrayFlowHint: Only for TCP + Reality/TLS. Leave empty for WebSocket/gRPC/XHTTP.
nodes.sniScanHint: Scans the /24 subnet of the node IP for hosts that support TLS 1.3 + H2 — suitable as Reality destination & SNI.
nodes.sniScanNoResults: No suitable SNI hosts found in this subnet.
nodes.sniScanNeighborHint: Same /24 subnet as your node — ideal for Reality
nodes.sniScanOtherHint: Domain's real IP is outside the scanned subnet
nodes.xrayShortIdsHint: Comma-separated hex strings (0-16 chars). Empty string is valid.
nodes.xrayWsHostHint: For CDN (Cloudflare). Leave empty if not using CDN.
nodes.xrayXhttpHostHint: For CDN. Leave empty if not using CDN.
nodes.xrayXhttpModeHint: auto (recommended), packet-up (for strict CDN), stream-up (for H2/H3)
nodes.xrayApiPortHint: Local port for management API (not public, default 61000)
nodes.portConflictDesc: Port 443/80 is used by the panel (Caddy). Xray on the same server must use a different port (e.g. 8443). Otherwise Xray will break the panel after a server reboot.
nodes.obfsPasswordHint: Same password must be set on both server and client.
nodes.statsSecretAutoHint: Leave empty — a secret will be generated automatically.
nodes.confirmDeleteCascade: Delete cascade link "{name}"? This will also undeploy it.
nodes.xrayGenerateKeysConfirm: Generate new x25519 Reality keys via SSH? This will overwrite existing keys.

users.title: Users
users.groupsHint: User will have access to nodes from these groups. If not selected — access to all nodes without groups.
users.expireAtPastError: Expiration date/time cannot be in the past
users.confirmDeleteFull: Are you sure you want to delete this user?
users.noGroupsAccess: No groups (access to all nodes without groups)

groups.title: Server Groups
groups.pageHint: Groups allow flexible control of user access to servers
groups.formHint: Name in app — displayed in subscription list for clients. Device limit = 0 means unlimited.
groups.confirmDelete: Delete group {name}? It will be unlinked from all nodes and users.
groups.noGroups: No groups yet. Create the first group above.
groups.noGroupsHint: Groups help organize servers by regions, plans, or other criteria.

settings.title: Settings
settings.loadBalancingHint: Less loaded nodes will be first in subscription
settings.hideOverloadedHint: Nodes with online ≥ limit won't be included in subscription
settings.gracePeriodHint: How long to remember IP after disconnection. Protects from false triggers when switching WiFi↔LTE
settings.howItWorksDesc: Devices are counted by unique IP addresses. If user connects from new IP and old one was active less than {minutes} minutes ago — both IPs count toward the limit.
settings.subscriptionsTTLHint: Ready configs. Reset on node changes
settings.onlineSessionsTTLHint: For device limit. Less = more accurate
settings.subscriptionsPerMinuteHint: Protection from token brute force. Norm: 60-100
settings.sshPoolHint: Reuses SSH connections for faster operations. Saves ~200-500ms per operation.
settings.sshPoolKeepaliveHint: Send keepalive packets to prevent NAT timeout
settings.nodeAuthInsecureHint: Enable if panel uses HTTP or self-signed SSL. Nodes will accept any certificate when connecting to auth API. Disable for production with valid SSL.
settings.totpEnableHint: Confirm your current password, then connect the new device with a QR code on the next screen.
settings.totpProtectedHint: Confirm your password here, then enter the current TOTP code on the next screen to disable or rotate.
settings.totpEnableVerifyDescription: Scan the QR code and enter the code from your authenticator app to finish enabling TOTP.
settings.totpRotateVerifyDescription: Scan the new QR code and enter the code from your new device to complete secret rotation.
settings.passwordTotpNextStepHint: After password verification, you will be asked for the current TOTP code on a separate screen.
settings.resetStatsDesc: Delete all historical statistics data (online, traffic, nodes charts). This action is irreversible!
settings.resetStatsConfirm:
⚠️ Delete all statistics history?

This will clear all charts and tables on the Statistics page.

Continue?
settings.resetTrafficDesc: Resets used traffic counter (tx/rx) for all users. This action is irreversible!
settings.resetConfirm:
⚠️ WARNING!

Are you sure you want to reset traffic counter for ALL users?

This action is irreversible!
settings.resetConfirmFinal:
Final confirmation.

Counters tx/rx will be reset for all users.

Continue?
settings.autoBackupHint: Automatically create database backups on schedule
settings.keepLastHint: How many local backups to keep (old ones are deleted automatically)
settings.s3EnabledHint: Additionally send backups to S3-compatible storage
settings.s3KeepLastHint: How many backups to keep in S3
settings.restoreConfirm:
Restore from this backup?

⚠️ Current data will be replaced!
settings.hintLoadBalancing: Sort nodes by current load in subscriptions
settings.hintHideOverloaded: Don't show nodes that reached max online users
settings.flushCacheDesc: Clears all cached data in Redis (subscriptions, users, sessions). May temporarily increase server load.
settings.flushCacheConfirm:
Clear all cached data?

This will temporarily increase server load as cache rebuilds.
settings.subLogoUrlHint: Link to an image (PNG/SVG/WebP). Leave empty to use the default icon.
settings.subPageTitleHint: Text above the logo on the subscription page. Default: "Connection".
settings.subSupportUrlHint: Shown as a "Support" button in Hiddify. Example: https://t.me/yoursupport
settings.subWebPageUrlHint: Shown as an "Open Web Page" button in Hiddify. Example: landing or user portal.
settings.subHappProviderIdHint: Optional 8-character code from happ-proxy.com. Unlocks statistics and feedback button.
settings.subUpdateIntervalHint: How often clients auto-refresh the subscription. Sent as the Profile-Update-Interval response header. Range: 1–168 hours.
settings.subButtonsHint: Use {url} in the URL field to insert the subscription link (e.g. hiddify://import/{url}). For Shadowrocket use sub://{url_b64}. For query params use {url_encoded}. Icons: tabler.io/icons (e.g. ti-download).
settings.subHiddifyProfileHint: The links below appear in Hiddify / Clash as profile card buttons.

setup.title: Initial Setup
setup.credentialsEncrypted: Login and password will be stored encrypted.
setup.enableTotpHint: After account creation you will need to confirm setup in an authenticator app.
setup.noRecoveryWarning: Version 1 has no self-service recovery. Save the secret in a safe place.

backup.confirmRestore: This will replace all data. Continue?

outbounds.title: Traffic Filtering
outbounds.pageDesc: Rules determine what to block, allow, or redirect through a proxy.
outbounds.resetDesc: Remove all rules, return to default
outbounds.rulesHint: One rule per line. Two built-in actions: reject(…) — block, direct(…) — allow through server. Rules apply top to bottom, first match wins.
outbounds.aclInlineDisabled: ACL inline editor is disabled: ACL is turned off in node settings.
outbounds.aclInlineDisabledFile: ACL inline editor is disabled: ACL source type is set to file mode in node settings.
outbounds.noProxyWarning: Proxy exists but no rules — proxy won't be used. Add a rule like:
outbounds.proxyChipsHint: Your proxies — click to insert into rule:
outbounds.defaultRulesWarning: If you set rules, server defaults (reject(geoip:cn), reject(geoip:private)) will be disabled. Add them manually if needed.
outbounds.proxyServersHint: for routing traffic through SOCKS5/HTTP proxy
outbounds.proxyDesc: Only needed if you want to route some traffic through your own proxy server. Add proxy here, then use its name in rules above: proxy_name(condition)

stats.title: Statistics
stats.hint: Data updates every 5 minutes. Select a period to view history.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.name: Name
apiKeys.allowedIPs: Allowed IPs (one per line, empty = all)
apiKeys.empty: No API keys yet. Create one to enable external integrations.
apiKeys.revealWarning: Copy this key now. It will never be shown again.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.secretPlaceholder: Leave empty to skip signature verification
webhooks.secretHint: Verify via X-Webhook-Signature header: sha256=HMAC-SHA256(secret, timestamp.body)

network.title: Network Map
network.fillRequired: Please fill in all required fields
network.noNodesOrLinks: No nodes or cascade links yet. Add nodes and create links.
network.confirmDeleteLink: Delete this cascade link? This will also undeploy it.
network.dragToCreateHint: Drag from any node to another to create a cascade link
network.betaDescription: Verify the resulting route, hop status, and exit IP after every deploy or sync.
network.modeReverseFlow: Traffic path: Client → Portal → Bridge → Internet. The tunnel is initiated by the Bridge.
network.modeForwardFlow: Traffic path: Client → Portal → (Relay →) Bridge → Internet. All nodes need a public IP.
network.modeForwardWarning: Forward Chain requires all hops to have a public IP. Verify the exit node after each sync.
network.modeReverseWarning: After deploy, confirm the tunnel is up and traffic exits from the expected Bridge node.
network.tunnelPortReverseHint: In Reverse mode this port is listened on the Portal node.
network.tunnelPortForwardHint: In Forward Chain this port is listened on the Bridge/Relay side of this hop.
network.realityTunnelHint: These REALITY settings apply only to the node-to-node tunnel, not to the client-facing inbound of the Portal node.
network.portConflictClientHint: This tunnel port is already used by another active link on the listening side.
network.modeReverseHint: Bridge initiates tunnel to Portal. Bridge can be behind NAT.
network.modeForwardHint: Portal connects to Bridge directly. All nodes need public IP.
network.confirmDeployChain: Deploy entire chain? This will sync all connected nodes in the correct order.
network.internetDesc: Traffic exits to the Internet from connected nodes

mcp.title: MCP Integration
mcp.subtitle: Connect AI assistants to manage your panel
mcp.motto: Censor uses AI to block? Time to get your own.
mcp.claudeConfigPath: Add to your Claude Desktop config file:
mcp.cursorConfigPath: Add to .cursor/mcp.json in your project root:
mcp.customConfigDesc: Any HTTP client with SSE support:
mcp.keyModalDesc: Creates a key with mcp:enabled + all read scopes.
mcp.writeAccessHint: Select additional write permissions (read-only by default)
mcp.toolQuery: Read data (users, nodes, groups, stats, logs)
mcp.toolExecuteSsh: Execute SSH command on a node
mcp.promptOverview: Full panel overview: nodes, users, health
mcp.promptUserReport: Detailed report for a specific user
mcp.promptSetupNode: Interactive guide to add a new node
mcp.promptExpiredUsers: Find and manage expired user accounts
